'''Brodo Asogi''', also known as the '''Green Planet''',<ref name="twitter">{{Twitter|starwars|status/305425618542153729|[[StarWars.com]]|quote=Good to know the Green Planet was well repped in the Galactic Senate (E.T.'s home planet)|archivedate=20190723164729}}</ref> was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Asogian/Legends|Asogian]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. It was located near the border of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], nearby [[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]] and [[Huk/Legends|Huk]].<ref name="TEA" />

It was represented by [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Grebleips/Legends|Grebleips]] and two aides in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. According to the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]], Grebleips was to fund a expedition project [[Extragalactic/Legends|outside]] [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] shortly before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="hnn">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|50||Other Headlines}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Yes, there it was—home, planet of many names, named by many ages—Vomestra, Brodo Asogi, Od-Di-Pa 5, Tum Lux O-ty, Alata Zerka, all of which translated to—the Green Planet. As Earth was called the Blue Planet for its waters, so E.T.'s home was called the Green Planet for its plant life, which flourished here as on no other planet in the galaxy.|''E.T.: The Book of the Green Planet''|<ref name="Book">{{BookCite|author=William Kotzwinkle|title=E.T.: The Book of the Green Planet|publisher=G.P. Putnam's Sons|isbn=0-399-13063-2}}</ref>}}
[[File:GreenPlanet.jpg|thumb|A Child of the Green Planet.]]
In [[Wikipedia:E.T.: The Book of the Green Planet|''E.T.: The Book of the Green Planet'']], a novel based on a story by [[Steven Spielberg]] written by William Kotzwinkle and illustrated by David Wiesner in [[1985]], ''Brodo Asogi'' was one of many names given to E.T.'s homeworld, along with ''Vomestra'', ''Od-Di-Pa 5'', ''Tum Lux O-Ty'', and ''Alata Zerka''—all of which meant "Green Planet" in one language or another.<ref name="Book" />
